evaluator: fresh independent Level 2 evaluator (per-slice), then a later full-epic Level 2 close-out owns the passes flip
approval_notes: Orchestrator-directed F006 Slice 1 (the Catalogue query API: search + category matrix + compare). Build ONE slice, open ONE PR, do NOT merge, do NOT flip any passes flag. F006 stays passes:false.

Extends the F005 read-only /catalogue API (router/queries/service/schemas + confidence) with three new GET capabilities. No migration (Alembic head stays 0007), no new runtime dependency, no persistence-semantics change, no LLM.

Owner decisions binding this slice: Q3=A search is deterministic in-DB ILIKE (no Postgres extension / no FTS index, deferred to F008); Q6 build provider-agnostic and prove multi-provider via SYNTHETIC fixtures that are never published on a normal stack run; Q7 quota-unit normalization is conservative + stdlib-only and FAILS CLOSED on anything it cannot confidently normalize; Q8 no new Python/npm runtime dependency; Q4/Q2 read-only, no LLM, no new table.

Standing autonomy boundaries: may branch/implement/test/commit/push/open ONE PR; may NOT merge, flip any passes flag, add a dependency, add a migration, weaken any existing test/fixture/CHECK/trigger, touch trg_offer_version_immutable or the two 0006 separation triggers, add a mutation endpoint, expose community/discovery_candidate data, or add an LLM/network/user-controlled-URL path.
objective: Extend the read-only catalogue HTTP API with three new GET capabilities over the PUBLISHED catalogue: (a) GET /catalogue/search?q=&provider=&category=&zero_cost_class=&offer_type=&commercial_use=&status=&page= — keyword ILIKE search + composable filters with deterministic total ordering and stable pagination; (b) GET /catalogue/categories — the canonical 14-category taxonomy crossed with each provider's coverage state (verified_free / no_free_tier / not_offered), plus an honest per-provider uncategorized rollup for published services with no category assigned; (c) GET /catalogue/compare?offers=1,2,3 — a normalized side-by-side of a bounded id set showing Z0 class, quotas with NORMALIZED units (failing closed to 'unknown' when a unit cannot be confidently normalized), requires_card/paid-dependency, commercial/personal use, the plain-language confidence LABEL, completeness/freshness, and evidence linkage. All read-only, GET-only, parameterized, with no SSRF surface; community/discovery_candidate data is never surfaced; confidence stays LABEL-primary with the numeric score only in a nested advanced{} block.
scope: apps/api/app/read_api/taxonomy.py (new): the canonical 14-category taxonomy (slug + name + ordinal) from docs/PRODUCT_REQUIREMENTS.md as a pure code constant. No DB seed, no migration.

apps/api/app/read_api/normalize.py (new): conservative, stdlib-only quota-unit normalization shared helper (Slice 3 adviser will reuse it). Normalizes known data-size units (decimal B/KB/MB/GB/TB and binary KiB/MiB/GiB/TiB) to a canonical base; returns an explicit 'cannot normalize' / unknown result for anything else (fail closed). Never guesses a conversion.

apps/api/app/read_api/search.py (new): input validation + deterministic SELECT builder for search. Published-only (EXISTS an offer_version); parameterized ILIKE on a bounded q over provider name/slug + service canonical_name + offer_type + zero_cost_class; composable equality filters (provider slug, category slug, zero_cost_class, offer_type, commercial_use, status); deterministic total ordering (provider_slug, service canonical_name, offer_id); fixed page size with stable pagination. Never queries candidate/discovery_candidate.

apps/api/app/read_api/queries.py: add read-only query helpers for search (paged published-offer query + total count), the category coverage matrix (published services grouped by provider x category_id), and compare (batch fetch published offers by id). Pure SELECTs; no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE.

apps/api/app/read_api/service.py: add serializers for the search page response, the category matrix response, and the compare response (reusing the existing label-primary/advanced serialization and quota serialization; quotas additionally annotated with normalized units in compare).

apps/api/app/read_api/schemas.py: add Pydantic response models for search (page + pagination meta), the category matrix (taxonomy entries x provider coverage + uncategorized rollup), and compare (per-offer normalized comparison rows). Confidence stays label-primary; numeric only in advanced{}.

apps/api/app/read_api/router.py: register the three new GET routes with validated query parameters (bounded q length; provider/category slug pattern; enum-constrained filters; page>=1; bounded compare id set), returning 422 for malformed input and 404 for unknown identifiers. No mutating verb is added.

tests/unit/test_read_api_search.py (new): offline deterministic tests for normalize.py (known units normalize; unknown units fail closed), the search query builder + validation (keyword match, each filter, filters compose, deterministic ordering + pagination, hostile q neutralized, bad slug/enum -> 422), the category matrix serialization (14 canonical categories always present; coverage states across MULTIPLE synthetic providers; uncategorized rollup), and compare serialization (normalized parity; unknown units fail closed; oversize set rejected; unknown id -> 404). Includes a guard test that no candidate/discovery field appears in any new response.

tests/integration/test_read_api_search.py (new): DATABASE_URL-gated tests that run against the live schema (migrations 0001..0007) inside a rolled-back transaction. Publishes the real Cloudflare fixture catalogue AND inserts clearly-synthetic multi-provider categorized offers (fixture-only, rolled back) to prove search across >1 provider, the category coverage matrix across providers/categories, and compare across providers end-to-end.
tests/fixtures (as needed): synthetic 'example-*' provider fixture data/helpers used only by the tests above; clearly synthetic and never published on a normal stack run.
docs/ARCHITECTURE.md: document the new read-only search / category-matrix / compare endpoints (parameterized, GET-only, published-only, no SSRF). Append an agent-state/progress.md handoff.
out_of_scope: The architecture adviser / 'chat with your data' / any LLM path (later F006 slices).
Any Postgres full-text-search index or extension (Q3=A: deferred to F008); any new migration (head stays 0007).
Any new Python or npm runtime dependency (requirements-sync + npm audit stay green; stdlib + already-present deps only). If a hard blocker requires one, STOP and report.
Any write/mutation endpoint; any change to publish/classify/ingest semantics; assigning category_id during publish; seeding the category table.
Weakening OfferVersion immutability (trg_offer_version_immutable) or the two 0006 separation triggers.
Exposing community / discovery_candidate data in any response.
Any user-controlled URL / fetchable host (no SSRF surface).
Flipping F006 passes:true or editing any feature_list.json passes flag.
acceptance_criteria: SEARCH: returns the correct PUBLISHED offers for a keyword and for each individual filter (provider, category, zero_cost_class, offer_type, commercial_use, status); filters compose (AND); ordering is deterministic and pagination is stable across pages.

CATEGORY MATRIX: GET /catalogue/categories returns all 14 canonical categories crossed with provider coverage states, plus an honest per-provider uncategorized rollup; multi-provider coverage is demonstrated.

COMPARE: GET /catalogue/compare?offers=... returns a normalized side-by-side for a bounded id set (Z0 class, normalized quotas, requires_card/paid-deps, commercial/personal use, confidence LABEL, completeness/freshness, evidence linkage); a unit that cannot be confidently normalized fails closed to 'unknown' (never a guessed conversion).
GET-only enforced: POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE on the new routes -> 405.

Input safety: hostile q (URL/host, SQL-ish, oversize, path-traversal) is neutralized via parameterized bounded queries (no SSRF, no injection); a bad slug -> 422; url-like/traversal slug -> 404/422; unknown identifier -> 404; an oversize compare id set -> 422/400.
community/discovery_candidate data never appears in any new response (guard test present).
OfferVersion immutability + both 0006 separation triggers remain intact and untouched.
Multi-provider behaviour is proven via synthetic fixtures (search across >1 provider; category matrix across providers; compare across providers) that are never published on a normal stack run.
No new dependency and no migration: Alembic head stays 0007; requirements-sync stays green; confidence stays LABEL-primary with the numeric score only in advanced{}.
Regression green offline: scripts/test.ps1 and scripts/check.ps1 -NodeAudit (all 8 gates) pass.
Live stack returns real results from the new endpoints (direct API port + through the nginx /api proxy), including the guardrail probes.
F006 remains passes:false; a fresh-context independent Level 2 evaluator owns any status change.

integration_commands: Fresh live stack on alternate host ports (POSTGRES_PORT=55432, API_PORT=8010, WEB_PORT=8090, DATABASE_URL=postgresql+psycopg://atlas:atlas@localhost:55432/atlas): docker compose down --volumes -> scripts/stack-up.ps1 --build (fresh DB applies 0001-0007) -> python -m app.ingest.runner cloudflare.example.yaml --fixtures tests/fixtures/ingest/cloudflare/html --publish -> scripts/stack-smoke.ps1 -> DATABASE_URL set, run pytest tests/integration/test_read_api_search.py (synthetic multi-provider proof, rolled back) -> curl the new /catalogue/search, /catalogue/categories, /catalogue/compare endpoints direct on API_PORT and through the nginx /api proxy on WEB_PORT, including the guardrail probes (writes->405, hostile q, bad slug->422, unknown->404, oversize compare->422/400) -> docker compose down --volumes. Delete scratch files; leave git status clean.

risks.data_migration: None: Slice 1 adds NO migration. Alembic head stays 0007. The category taxonomy is a code constant, not a DB seed.
risks.security_privacy: All new endpoints are GET-only and parameterized. q is length-bounded and only ever used in a parameterized ILIKE (never fetched, never string-formatted into SQL); slug/enum/int params are validated (bad -> 422, unknown -> 404). No user-controlled URL / no SSRF surface. Only published catalogue data is read; candidate/discovery_candidate tables are never queried; a guard test asserts those fields never surface.
risks.portability: No new runtime dependency; stdlib-only normalization; reuses SQLAlchemy + FastAPI + Pydantic already present. amd64/arm64 unaffected.
risks.zero_cost_safety: The API surfaces the Z0 class, reasons, and confidence exactly as persisted; it never re-derives or upgrades a class. Compare normalization never fabricates a value: an un-normalizable unit fails closed to 'unknown'. Coverage states derive strictly from published data; unassigned categories are reported honestly, never guessed.
risks.immutability: Read-only slice: no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE in application code. trg_offer_version_immutable and the two 0006 separation triggers are untouched. (Integration tests may INSERT synthetic rows inside a transaction that is rolled back; they never UPDATE/DELETE a version and never commit.)
risks.regression: Additive endpoints + new modules; the seven existing /catalogue endpoints, their queries/serializers, and all existing tests are unchanged. No behaviour of publish/classify/ingest changes.
risks.supply_chain: No new Python or npm runtime dependency; requirements-sync + npm audit stay green; stdlib + already-present deps only.

notes: Orchestrator directed F006 Slice 1: the read-only Catalogue query API (search + category matrix + compare), then STOP for a fresh-context Level 2 evaluation. Do NOT flip passes; do NOT merge; do NOT add a migration or a dependency; GET-only; no SSRF; never expose candidate/discovery data; conservative fail-closed quota normalization; confidence stays label-primary; do NOT weaken immutability/separation.
